subject: DevOps
title: Terraform Workspaces
description: A quick win: understand Terraform Workspaces.

hook.text: You have one set of Terraform code. You need to manage dev, staging, and production from it. Workspaces help.

buildup.text: A workspace is a separate state file for the same code. Switch workspace, switch environment. Same code, new state.

discovery.text: terraform workspace new staging creates a fresh state. Resources in staging don't interfere with production.

twist.text: Some teams prefer separate directories over workspaces. Directories are more explicit but require duplicated code.

climax.text: Terraform Cloud takes workspaces further: remote execution, role-based access, and policy enforcement per workspace.

punchline.text: Workspaces isolate state per environment. Same code, separate realities.

quiz.question: What does a Terraform workspace provide?
options: A separate state file for the same code, isolating environments
A graphical editor for Terraform files
A way to run multiple Terraform versions simultaneously
